text.title: Cassandra Wide-Column Modeling Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real nosql-cassandra-patterns deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Partition key is timestamp / auto-increment ID alone; sequential writes hit one token range; cluster hot-spots.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated Cassandra like a wide-column relational DB.
antipattern.fix: Combine high-cardinality entity ID with time bucket: (entity_id, date) — keeps partition size bounded.

antipattern.symptom: Query uses ALLOW FILTERING; latency degrades from sub-ms to seconds as the table grows.
antipattern.root-cause: Author added ALLOW FILTERING to bypass missing access pattern.
antipattern.fix: Design table-per-access-pattern; reject ALLOW FILTERING in CI.

antipattern.symptom: Secondary index on UUID column; queries fan out to every node; latency worse than full table scan.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to relational-style secondary indexes.
antipattern.fix: Use materialized views or denormalised tables; avoid secondary indexes on high-cardinality columns.

antipattern.symptom: Every write uses INSERT IF NOT EXISTS; latency is 4× regular write due to Paxos.
antipattern.root-cause: Author applied LWT broadly to avoid race conditions.
antipattern.fix: LWT only for true compare-and-swap; use COUNTER columns for atomic increment; document why LWT is required when used.
